observations: SCOPE-DRIFT CHECK (programmatic, byte-level diff against round 0): reconstructed the round-0 contract verbatim (from this evaluator's own round-0 Read) and diffed it field-by-field against revision 1. Confirmed: 'scope', 'non_goals', 'estimated_lines' (260), 'evidence_dir', and 'evidence_convention' are all byte-identical to round 0. Of the 22 verification entries, EXACTLY entries 5, 11, and 12 changed; all other 19 (1-4, 6-10, 13-22) are byte-identical to round 0. design_notes retained all seven round-0 keys unchanged and gained exactly one new key ('revision_1_content_honesty_fix'). The only new top-level key is 'approvals' (bookkeeping). This is precisely the narrow, targeted fix this evaluator's round-0 required_fix asked for -- no unrelated scope drift.

ROUND-0 HIGH FINDING RE-VERIFIED CLOSED: re-ran this evaluator's own round-0 mutants/mutant_fabricated.py (honest blocking/exception/exit-code mechanics; fabricated 'cafef00d...' SHA in repository_references/framework_references/planning_references/evidence_references; hardcoded stale '2020-01-01T00:00:00Z' timestamp; ci_references carrying a fabricated 'run_id'/'status':'verified-green' claim with NO honest note) against the revised 22-entry suite on the same disposable clone (real HEAD 17408d942cb09acced7c0874cd4bd363dce3bfc5): it now FAILS EXACTLY entries 5, 11, and 12 (all other 19 entries unaffected), with named assertion mismatches -- entry 5: ('repository_references revision mismatch', 'cafef00dcafef00dcafef00dcafef00dcafef00d', '17408d942cb09acced7c0874cd4bd363dce3bfc5'); entries 11 and 12 report the identical mismatch shape against their own independently-recomputed disposable-fixture HEAD (e.g. '4232da3453d31bf29ad7c8839c1807950ab99c7d'), confirming the fix anchors against the FIXTURE's real HEAD, not accidentally the outer clone's HEAD. An independently-authored, unmodified honest reference implementation (mutants/base_honest.py, unchanged from round 0) passes all 22 entries cleanly with the new assertions active, confirming no false-fail was introduced.

TIMESTAMP BOUNDARY PROBED, PRECISE AT 600s: built two new adversarial mutants offsetting captured_at by a fixed skew computed at write time. A 590-seconds-stale timestamp correctly PASSES entries 5/11/12 (inside the window); a 610-seconds-stale timestamp correctly FAILS all three with the exact delta named (e.g. 'captured_at is stale or not close to the actual run clock', ..., 610.09...). The freshness bound is a genuine, precise cutoff, not a loose approximation.

BOTH ANCHOR FIELDS INDEPENDENTLY COVERED: built a new adversarial mutant honest on repository_references (real HEAD) but fabricating framework_references ONLY (unrelated hardcoded 'deadbeef...' SHA). All three revised entries correctly and independently FAIL on 'framework_references revision mismatch', proving the anchoring assertion checks framework_references and repository_references as two independent assertions, not merely one field standing in for both.

HONEST NOTE CANNOT LAUNDER A SMUGGLED CI CLAIM: built a new adversarial mutant carrying BOTH the genuine, promised honest limitation note AND a smuggled fabricated 'run_id'/'status':'verified-green' claim in the same ci_references item. All three revised entries still correctly FAIL on the run_id assertion, confirming the presence of an honest note does not exempt the artifact from the 'no fabricated run_id/verified-green' checks -- the two assertions are independent, not either/or.

ALL SIX ROUND-0 ADVERSARIAL MUTANTS STILL DIE AT THEIR INTENDED ENTRIES, RE-CONFIRMED WITH PROPER PER-ENTRY CLONE ISOLATION (git clean -fdx + checkout before each of the 22 commands, to rule out any cross-entry state leakage as a confound): mutant_always_pass.py (always-exit-0/copied-fixture) now caught at [5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 15] -- gaining an EXPECTED additional catch at entry 5, since its hardcoded baseline content also now fails the new content-honesty assertions there, a strict coverage improvement, not a regression. mutant_dirty_bypass.py (dirty-tracked-state ignored) still caught at [7, 15], unchanged. mutant_precedence.py (blocking downgraded to exception under any --tolerate-untracked flag) still caught ONLY at [15], unchanged -- entry 15 remains the sole defense for this defect class (see round-0 observation, still non-blocking, still worth awareness). mutant_smuggle_pending.py (fabricated schema-valid operator_approval smuggled on the pending path) still caught only at [10], unchanged. mutant_usage_error_wrong_code.py still caught only at [14], unchanged. mutant_partial_ref_check.py still caught only at [9], unchanged. No prior kill shot was weakened or lost by this revision.

BONUS PROBE (not required, run for extra rigor): mutants/mutant_side_effect.py (leaks a stray 'collection-log.txt' write into --repo-root) was re-run with proper clone isolation and is caught at [5, 6, 10, 11, 12, 13] -- entry 6 (output isolation, its originally-intended catch) is confirmed still firing; the additional catches at 5/10/11/12/13 are an incidental, expected side-effect of the leak itself becoming a new untracked blocking finding that flips several fixtures' own verdicts, not a new coverage claim being made by the reviewer.

OTHER 19 ENTRIES CONFIRMED FUNCTIONALLY UNCHANGED (behavioral re-run, not just text-diff): re-ran the full 22-entry suite against the honest reference implementation with proper clean-between-entries isolation -- 22/22 PASS, matching round 0's result for entries 1-4, 6-10, 13-22 exactly (same PASS outcomes), confirming the revision's byte-for-byte-unchanged entries also behave identically, not merely read identically.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: `git status --porcelain` checked before and after every probe in this round. The real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework shows only the pre-existing untracked entries (.agent/contracts/041.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) plus this review's own two artifacts (.agent/qa/041-contract-review.json from round 0, .agent/qa/041-contract-review-rev1.json from this round), unchanged throughout. All mutant construction, the two new probes (timestamp-boundary, partial-anchoring, honest-note-plus-smuggled-claim), and all re-verification ran only against the same disposable `git clone` used in round 0 (.../scratchpad/041review/clone1, real HEAD 17408d942cb09acced7c0874cd4bd363dce3bfc5) and its own ephemeral `mktemp -d` sub-fixtures.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1, fit for implementation as-is. This evaluator's round-0 single HIGH finding (baseline.json content was schema-valid but never truth-checked against real repository state) is genuinely and robustly closed: entries 5, 11, and 12 were extended, and only those three, with independently-recomputed real-HEAD anchoring for both framework_references and repository_references, a genuine honest ci_references note assertion paired with an explicit rejection of fabricated run_id/verified-green claims, and a 600-second captured_at freshness bound. A programmatic byte-diff confirms this revision is exactly this narrow fix -- scope, non_goals, estimated_lines, and the other 19 verification entries are unchanged. Empirically re-proven: this evaluator's own round-0 fabrication mutant now fails exactly entries 5/11/12 with named SHA/timestamp/CI-claim mismatches (not the outer clone's HEAD, but each fixture's own independently-recomputed HEAD); an unmodified honest reference implementation passes all 22 entries cleanly. Three additional adversarial probes constructed this round were all correctly caught: a 610-second-stale timestamp (589-second-stale correctly still passes, confirming a precise 600s cutoff, not a loose approximation); a mutant honest on repository_references but fabricating framework_references alone (both fields are independently checked, not one standing in for the other); and a mutant carrying a genuine honest note ALONGSIDE a smuggled verified-green claim (the honest note does not launder the smuggled claim -- both assertions are independent). All six of this evaluator's round-0 adversarial mutants were re-run against the revised suite with strict per-entry clone isolation and still die at exactly their originally-intended entries (mutant_always_pass.py gains one strictly-additive new catch at entry 5, a coverage improvement, not a regression); entry 15 remains the sole, non-blocking-but-noted defense for the blocking-downgraded-under-any-tolerate-flag precedence class, unchanged from round 0. Fit for implementation as-is.
